findings.reachability: Authenticated admin-level user (capability: edit_pages). The chart settings form is admin-only, so this is an admin-to-admin stored XSS (lower severity than subscriber-reachable).
findings.justification: The _sanitize() method stores raw POST values for 'target', 'separator', and 'chart' keys with no HTML or JavaScript escaping; these are later echoed into a <script> block via bare json_encode() (without JSON_HEX_TAG | JSON_HEX_AMP), which does not escape '<', '>', or '&', allowing a stored XSS payload such as </script><script>alert(1)</script> to break out of the JSON literal.

detection_pattern.section_name: Stored XSS: json_encode without JSON_HEX_TAG in script context
detection_pattern.description: Finds PHP files that echo json_encode() of a variable directly into HTML/JS context without JSON_HEX_TAG or WordPress's wp_json_encode() wrapper (which adds that flag), potentially allowing script-breaking characters to create XSS.

findings.reachability: Any authenticated admin hitting the chart admin page with a crafted formkey GET parameter.
findings.justification: $_GET['formkey'] is stored directly into $this->formkey at line 32 without sanitize_key() or sanitize_text_field(); although a whitelist check at line 36 prevents out-of-whitelist values from proceeding, the unsanitized value is concatenated into the option name at line 119 and into admin_url() at line 124 with no escaping, violating defense-in-depth.

detection_pattern.section_name: Missing input sanitization: raw $_GET stored to property
detection_pattern.description: Catches files where a $_GET superglobal value is assigned to a variable or property directly without any sanitization function, which is required by WordPress coding standards and is a prerequisite for injection/XSS chains.

findings.reachability: The constructor is gated by current_user_can('edit_pages') in the calling code (mw-wp-form.php line 78), so the effective reach is admin-only. However, _save() itself performs no independent capability check.
findings.justification: _save() checks only the nonce (check_admin_referer) but omits current_user_can(), meaning any future code path that instantiates MW_WP_Form_Chart_Controller without the outer gate would allow lower-privileged users to write chart settings via update_option().

detection_pattern.section_name: Missing capability check: save handler relies on nonce only
detection_pattern.description: Finds admin POST handlers that call check_admin_referer() (CSRF check) but lack a co-located current_user_can() call; CSRF protection does not substitute for authorization — a nonce proves request origin, not user privilege.

findings.reachability: Authenticated admin who triggers the save action. The redirect destination includes $this->formkey which was validated against the whitelist, so exploitation requires first compromising the whitelist or filter.
findings.justification: wp_redirect() is called with a URL constructed from $this->formkey (set from $_GET, whitelisted) at line 124; if a filter hook 'mwform_contact_data_post_types' injects an unexpected value that passes the in_array check, the formkey could be manipulated to redirect to an off-site URL via protocol-relative or host injection.

detection_pattern.section_name: Open redirect: wp_redirect with user-controlled input
detection_pattern.description: Finds calls to wp_redirect() where the URL is built from user-controlled input ($_GET, $_POST, $_REQUEST) without wp_safe_redirect() or URL validation, enabling open redirect attacks.
